Streaming Infrastructure
AS
Audio Streaming StackView

A self-hostable delivery stack for broadcasters and audio products that need reliable ingest, failover, adaptive output, and operational visibility.

Get to a production-ready streaming baseline in days instead of months.
Developer API
MM
Media Metadata APIView

A unified API for reading, writing, and normalizing metadata across ingest, archive, and distribution workflows.

Stop maintaining one-off parsers and make metadata flows predictable.
Planning & Operations
BS
Broadcast SchedulerView

An open scheduling engine for live and automated radio playout that keeps planners, operators, and integrations aligned.

Replace brittle scheduling workflows with a system designed for modern broadcasting.

Streaming infrastructure project

Audio Streaming Stack

A resilient delivery baseline for teams that already know the cost of outage triage.

Audio Streaming Stack turns relay chains, failover rules, and playback outputs into one inspectable delivery surface that can ship quickly without feeling temporary.

Book a streaming reviewView all projects
Use when uptime risk is already an operator burden

Built for stations and platform teams that need failover, delivery visibility, and a deployment shape they can still reason about six months later.

Deployment dossierWhat this project stabilizes first.

The page foregrounds failure handling, output paths, and rollout posture before it asks anyone to care about implementation detail.

Category
Streaming Infrastructure
Best for
Broadcasters, community radio, and platform teams
Main outcome
Get to a production-ready streaming baseline in days instead of months.
Adoption model
Open source core with guided setup and support options
Delivery modesIcecast + HLS
Operational focusFailover first
Best forLive audio
Signal 01Source visibility before listener impact

Track ingest and relay health early enough to act before delivery problems become support tickets.

Observability
Signal 02Fallback logic designed for real incidents

Silence detection and switchover rules stay explicit instead of hiding inside ad hoc shell scripts.

Continuity
Signal 03One baseline for direct and adaptive playback

Support modern listening surfaces without splitting the operating model into separate stacks.

Distribution

Project premise

Move from improvised streaming plumbing to a delivery system operators can read quickly.

Audio Streaming Stack is designed for teams that need a clean first implementation boundary: dependable live delivery, explicit failure handling, and enough visibility to hand the system between engineers without folklore.

Where it fits best

Use it when the streaming layer is the urgent issue today, but the architecture still needs room to connect with scheduling and metadata work later.

01

Broadcast continuity

Keep primary ingest, fallback behavior, and listener-facing outputs within one operational frame.

02

Implementation posture

Adopt it as a self-hosted core with guided rollout help rather than a black-box managed service.

03

Expansion path

Layer in adjacent Sonicverse projects once the delivery baseline is stable and observable.

Capability depth

The core capabilities stay close to the incidents teams already recognize.

Each capability is framed around an operational job, so the page reads like a briefing for engineers and operators instead of a generic feature list.

01

Ingest and relay control

Model the source path clearly enough that relay failures, silence events, and downstream breakage stop feeling mysterious.

02

Playback coverage

Serve direct streams and HLS outputs from the same delivery baseline so product and broadcast needs stay aligned.

03

Resilience rules

Treat fallback as a first-class operating concern instead of a late-stage patch on top of a fragile relay chain.

04

Readable operations

Give the next engineer a system they can inspect, extend, and reason about without reverse-engineering hidden assumptions.

Operational notes

The shortest technical read before the walkthrough.

Deployment styleSelf-hosted core with guided rollout and support options.
Failure handlingFailover-first design with explicit continuity controls.
Playback outputsDirect streams and HLS from one operational baseline.
Architecture postureComposable with existing broadcast tooling and orchestration.

Choose the next read

Plan the rollout or open the adjacent project.

If live delivery is only one part of the broader stack refresh, the related projects below show where teams usually go next.

Related projectBroadcast Scheduler

Connect delivery reliability with scheduling control when the station workflow needs both layers to align.

Sonicverse is the modular audio operations platform for teams that want enterprise-grade workflows without proprietary lock-in.
